tools.name: apis.createApiCache
tools.description: Creates a cache for the GraphQL API.

tools.name: apis.createApiKey
tools.description: Creates a unique key that you can distribute to clients who invoke your API.

tools.name: apis.createFunction
tools.description:
Creates a Function object.

A function is a reusable entity. You can use multiple functions to compose the resolver logic.

tools.name: apis.createResolver
tools.description:
Creates a Resolver object.

A resolver converts incoming requests into a format that a data source can understand, and converts the data source's responses into GraphQL.

tools.name: apis.getIntrospectionSchema
tools.description: Retrieves the introspection schema for a GraphQL API.

tools.name: apis.getSchemaCreationStatus
tools.description: Retrieves the current status of a schema creation operation.

tools.name: apis.listApiKeys
tools.description:
Lists the API keys for a given API.

API keys are deleted automatically 60 days after they expire. However, they may still be included in the response until they have actually been deleted. You can safely call DeleteApiKey to manually delete a key before it's automatically deleted.

tools.name: apis.listDataSources
tools.description: Lists the data sources for a given API.

tools.name: apis.listResolvers
tools.description: Lists the resolvers for a given API and type.

tools.name: apis.listResolversByFunction
tools.description: List the resolvers that are associated with a specific function.

tools.name: apis.listTypes
tools.description: Lists the types for a given API.

tools.name: apis.startSchemaCreation
tools.description:
Adds a new schema to your GraphQL API.

This operation is asynchronous. Use to determine when it has completed.

tools.name: apis.updateApiCache
tools.description: Updates the cache for the GraphQL API.

tools.name: apis.updateApiKey
tools.description: Updates an API key. You can update the key as long as it's not deleted.

tools.name: dataplaneEvaluatecode.evaluateCode
tools.description: Evaluates the given code and returns the response. The code definition requirements depend on the specified runtime. For APPSYNC_JS runtimes, the code defines the request and response functions. The request function takes the incoming request after a GraphQL operation is parsed and converts it into a request configuration for the selected data source operation. The response function interprets responses from the data source and maps it to the shape of the GraphQL field output type.

tools.name: dataplaneEvaluatetemplate.evaluateMappingTemplate
tools.description:
Evaluates a given template and returns the response. The mapping template can be a request or response template.

Request templates take the incoming request after a GraphQL operation is parsed and convert it into a request configuration for the selected data source operation. Response templates interpret responses from the data source and map it to the shape of the GraphQL field output type.

Mapping templates are written in the Apache Velocity Template Language (VTL).

tools.name: domainnames.associateApi
tools.description: Maps an endpoint to your custom domain.

tools.name: domainnames.disassociateApi
tools.description: Removes an ApiAssociation object from a custom domain.

tools.name: tags.listTagsForResource
tools.description: Lists the tags for a resource.

tools.name: tags.tagResource
tools.description: Tags a resource with user-supplied tags.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
